Title: Repurposed inhibitor of bacterial dihydrodipicolinate reductase exhibits effective herbicidal activity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DHDPR; N-terminal Rossmann fold and C-terminal oligomerisation domain
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an intermediate required for lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Published maximal expression across 79 Arabidopsis organs and developmental stages is comparable or lower than several herbicide targets; specific tissue pattern not detailed in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for protein synthesis, carbon and nitrogen metabolism, and production of secondary metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In vitro target of 2,6-PDC; inhibition of this enzyme supports reduced Arabidopsis germination and growth via lysine biosynthesis blockade
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDHDPR1 was recombinantly produced and shown to be correctly folded. 2. It displayed DHDPR catalytic activity with substrate inhibition behavior. 3. Crystal structure (PDB 7T34) showed high similarity to AtDHDPR2 and a conserved 2,6-PDC binding pocket. 4. 2,6-PDC inhibited AtDHDPR1 with IC50 of 140 µM, supporting DHDPR as a herbicidal target in lysine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein expression; enzyme kinetics assay; inhibitor IC50 assay; X-ray crystallography; structural comparison; plant germination assay with inhibitor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,6-PDC treatment; Arabidopsis germination assays on agar and soil-grown plants under 16 h light/8 h dark at 22 °C
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct genetic tradeoffs not tested; chemical inhibition causes strong growth suppression/germination arrest
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents a promising herbicide target in lysine biosynthesis; directed evolution or engineering of inhibitor-resistant DHDPR variants in crops may support resistance breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein crystallized as a monomer in the asymmetric unit but predicted weak tetramer/dimer-of-dimers; substrate-binding loop appears flexible; PDB code 7T34

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DHDPR; N-terminal Rossmann fold and C-terminal oligomerisation domain
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an intermediate required for lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Published maximal expression across 79 Arabidopsis organs and developmental stages is comparable or lower than several herbicide targets; specific tissue pattern not detailed in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for protein synthesis, carbon and nitrogen metabolism, and production of secondary metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In vitro target of 2,6-PDC; inhibition of this enzyme supports reduced Arabidopsis germination and growth via lysine biosynthesis blockade
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDHDPR2 was recombinantly produced and shown to have similar folding to AtDHDPR1. 2. It is structurally highly similar to AtDHDPR1 and contains a conserved 2,6-PDC binding pocket. 3. 2,6-PDC inhibited AtDHDPR2 with IC50 of 470 µM, confirming it as a plant target of the herbicidal scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein expression; circular dichroism comparison; inhibitor IC50 assay; structural comparison to published crystal structure
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,6-PDC treatment; Arabidopsis germination assays on agar and soil-grown plants under 16 h light/8 h dark at 22 °C
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct genetic tradeoffs not tested; chemical inhibition causes strong growth suppression/germination arrest
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports DHDPR as a herbicide target; could inform engineering of inhibitor-resistant crop alleles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized Arabidopsis DHDPR isoform; crystal structure referenced as PDB 5UA0
